payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because it is the newer, current memory. mem_old was filtered out because it is superseded and therefore not treated as the authoritative record.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it is the current, non-superseded memory for Sam from Goldman, while mem_old was excluded as outdated and no longer authoritative.

entity_resolution.reason: The recall step preferred the latest valid memory and excluded the older one because it was superseded.

payload.intent: Explain why recall selected the newer memory for Sam from Goldman and filtered out the older one as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: Sam from Goldman is represented by the newer memory as the active/current record.
The older Sam-from-Goldman memory was filtered because it has been superseded and should not be treated as current.

details: No superseded fact presented as current.

repair_options: Keep mem_new as the current memory for Sam from Goldman.
Mark mem_old as superseded/non-current so it is filtered in future recall.
If needed, retain both only with explicit temporal versioning and a current-pointer to mem_new.
